017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.view.mustache;
018
019import java.io.*;
020
021import org.apache.juneau.http.*;
022import org.apache.juneau.http.response.*;
023import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.*;
024
025import com.github.mustachejava.*;
026
027/**
028 * Mixin that wires Mustache view-rendering onto any Juneau REST resource.
029 *
030 * <p>
031 * Compose into a host resource via
032 * {@link Rest#mixins() @Rest(mixins=MustacheMixin.class)}; the host then:
033 *
034 * <ol class='spaced-list'>
035 *    <li>Gains a default mount at {@code /mustache/*} that serves raw Mustache templates from the
036 *       importer's classpath by asking the configured {@link MustacheFactory} to compile and
037 *       render them with no scope (raw render path; callers who want attributes use
038 *       {@link MustacheView} from a typed handler instead).
039 *    <li>Picks up {@link MustacheViewRenderer} automatically via the mixin's
040 *       {@link Rest#responseProcessors() @Rest(responseProcessors=...)} declaration, so
041 *       {@code @RestOp}-method return values of type {@link MustacheView} render through the
042 *       Mustache engine without any additional wiring.
043 * </ol>
044 *
045 * <h5 class='figure'>Composition example (microservice):</h5>
046 *
047 * <p class='bjava'>
048 *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(path=<js>"/app"</js>, mixins=MustacheMixin.<jk>class</jk>)
049 *    <jk>public class</jk> AppResource <jk>extends</jk> RestServlet {
050 *
051 *       <ja>@Bean</ja> MustacheMixin mustache() {
052 *          <jk>return</jk> MustacheMixin.<jsm>create</jsm>()
053 *             .basePath(<js>"/templates/"</js>)
054 *             .templateSuffix(<js>".mustache"</js>)
055 *             .build();
056 *       }
057 *
058 *       <ja>@RestGet</ja>(<js>"/hello/{name}"</js>)
059 *       <jk>public</jk> View hello(<ja>@Path</ja> String <jv>name</jv>) {
060 *          <jk>return</jk> MustacheView.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"hello"</js>).attr(<js>"name"</js>, <jv>name</jv>);
061 *       }
062 *    }
063 * </p>
064 *
065 * <h5 class='section'>Configurable mount path:</h5>
066 *
067 * <p>
068 * The default mount {@code /mustache/*} can be overridden via the SVL variable
069 * {@code ${juneau.mustache.path:mustache}} &mdash; set via system property
070 * ({@code -Djuneau.mustache.path=views}), environment variable
071 * ({@code JUNEAU_MUSTACHE_PATH=views}), or {@code Config} key
072 * ({@code juneau.mustache.path = views}) to change the runtime mount without subclassing.
073 * Resolution happens once at {@link RestContext} construction time (SVL resolution in {@code @RestOp(path)}).
074 *
075 * <h5 class='section'>Mixin-only deployment:</h5>
076 *
077 * <p>
078 * This resource is designed for composition via {@code @Rest(mixins=...)}. The mount path is
079 * pinned at the op level by
080 * {@link RestGet @RestGet(path="/&#123;juneau.mustache.path:mustache&#125;/*")} on {@link #render};
081 * a class-level {@code @Rest(paths=...)} declaration would be silently ignored under the mixin
082 * pattern (see {@link Rest#paths() @Rest(paths)} Javadoc).
083 *
084 * <h5 class='section'>Choosing a MustacheFactory:</h5>
085 *
086 * <p>
087 * The {@code juneau-rest-server-view-mustache} module ships <b>only</b> the mustache.java
088 * compiler API in {@code provided} scope. <b>No factory bean is bundled.</b> The bridge resolves
089 * a {@link MustacheFactory} at first use from three sources, in order:
090 *
091 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
092 *    <li><b>User-supplied bean:</b> register a {@code @Bean MustacheFactory} (Spring) or
093 *       {@code BasicBeanStore.put(MustacheFactory.class, factory)} (microservice). Allows full
094 *       control of resolvers, object handlers, and encoders.
095 *    <li><b>Bridge default:</b> when nothing is registered, the bridge constructs a default
096 *       {@link DefaultMustacheFactory} anchored on a resource root derived from
097 *       {@link #getBasePath() basePath} (leading + trailing slashes trimmed; a {@code "/"} base
098 *       yields a no-prefix factory). With {@code basePath("/templates/")}, the factory resolves
099 *       template names like {@code "hello.mustache"} as classpath resource
100 *       {@code templates/hello.mustache}.
101 * </ul>
102 *
103 * <p>
104 * mustache.java's {@code DefaultMustacheFactory} caches compiled templates internally; the
105 * bridge does not currently expose a cache-disable knob (consumers who want hot-reload in
106 * development supply their own {@code @Bean MustacheFactory} configured as needed).
107 *
108 * <p>
109 * When no Mustache engine is on the classpath, the renderer surfaces
110 * {@link MustacheViewRenderer#NO_ENGINE_DIAGNOSTIC} naming the missing dependency.
111 *
112 * <h5 class='section'>Template suffix:</h5>
113 *
114 * <p>
115 * Unlike the Thymeleaf bridge (which always appends {@code .html} via the engine resolver's
116 * suffix setting), mustache.java has no resolver-suffix concept &mdash; the literal template name
117 * is what {@link MustacheFactory#compile(String) factory.compile(...)} sees. The
118 * {@link Builder#templateSuffix(String) templateSuffix(...)} builder knob fills the gap: when
119 * non-blank, the bridge appends the configured suffix to template names that don't already end
120 * with it (idempotent), so callers can write {@code MustacheView.of("hello")} or request
121 * {@code /mustache/hello} and have it resolve to {@code hello.mustache} on the classpath.
122 *
123 * <h5 class='section'>Multiple base paths:</h5>
124 *
125 * <p>
126 * A host with {@code /templates/} and {@code /admin/templates/} template trees registers two
127 * {@code MustacheMixin} beans, each mounted via a subclass declaring its own op-level
128 * {@code @RestGet(path=...)} override and its own {@code basePath}. See the
129 * {@code MustacheMixin_MockRest_Test} test in {@code juneau-integration-tests} for the canonical
130 * pattern.
131 *
132 * <h5 class='section'>OpenAPI surface:</h5>
133 *
134 * <p>
135 * The greedy {@code /*} handler is not API-meaningful and is excluded from generated
136 * Swagger / OpenAPI specs via {@link OpSwagger#ignore() @OpSwagger(ignore=true)}.
137 *
138 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
139 *    <li class='jc'>{@link MustacheView}
140 *    <li class='jc'>{@link MustacheViewRenderer}
141 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/MustacheViewSupport">Mustache View Support</a>
142 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerCompositionMixinsAndPaths">REST Server &mdash; Composition (mixins, paths)</a>
143 * </ul>
144 *
145 * @since 10.0.0
146 */
147// @formatter:off
148@Rest(
149   responseProcessors={MustacheViewRenderer.class}
150)
151public class MustacheMixin {
152
153   /** Default base path applied when no {@link Builder#basePath(String)} call has been made. */
154   public static final String DEFAULT_BASE_PATH = MustacheDispatcher.DEFAULT_BASE_PATH;
155
156   /** Default template suffix &mdash; empty (literal template names, no implicit suffix). */
157   public static final String DEFAULT_TEMPLATE_SUFFIX = MustacheDispatcher.DEFAULT_TEMPLATE_SUFFIX;
158
159   private final MustacheDispatcher worker;
160
161   /**
162    * Creates a new builder.
163    *
164    * @return A new builder.
165    */
166   public static Builder create() {
167      return new Builder();
168   }
169
170   /**
171    * No-arg constructor &mdash; uses {@link #DEFAULT_BASE_PATH} as the base path and
172    * {@link #DEFAULT_TEMPLATE_SUFFIX} for the template suffix.
173    *
174    * <p>
175    * The mixin walk falls back to this constructor when the
176    * {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.BeanStore BeanStore} does not have a registered
177    * {@code MustacheMixin} bean.
178    */
179   public MustacheMixin() {
180      this(create());
181   }
182
183   /**
184    * Builder constructor.
185    *
186    * @param builder The builder. Must not be {@code null}.
187    */
188   protected MustacheMixin(Builder builder) {
189      worker = builder.worker.build();
190   }
191
192   /**
193    * Returns the base path under which template resources are resolved.
194    *
195    * @return The base path. Never {@code null}.
196    */
197   public String getBasePath() {
198      return worker.getBasePath();
199   }
200
201   /**
202    * Returns the configured template-name suffix.
203    *
204    * @return The template suffix. Never {@code null}.
205    */
206   public String getTemplateSuffix() {
207      return worker.getTemplateSuffix();
208   }
209
210   /**
211    * Appends {@link #getTemplateSuffix()} to {@code name} if not already present (idempotent).
212    *
213    * @param name The template name. Must not be {@code null}.
214    * @return The template name with the configured suffix appended (if applicable).
215    */
216   public String applyTemplateSuffix(String name) {
217      return worker.applyTemplateSuffix(name);
218   }
219
220   /**
221    * Resolves the active {@link MustacheFactory} via the shared {@link MustacheDispatcher} worker.
222    *
223    * @param req The current REST request.
224    * @return The active Mustache factory. Never {@code null}.
225    */
226   public MustacheFactory resolveMustacheFactory(RestRequest req) {
227      return worker.resolveMustacheFactory(req);
228   }
229
230   /**
231    * [GET /mustache/*] &mdash; render a raw template through the Mustache engine.
232    *
233    * <p>
234    * The {@code @Path("/*") String path} captures the multi-segment trailing remainder (e.g. a
235    * request for {@code /mustache/about.mustache} matches the mount with
236    * {@code path = "about.mustache"}). Delegates to the shared {@link MustacheDispatcher} worker
237    * (path-traversal hardening, suffix application, factory resolution, and render).
238    *
239    * <p>
240    * Missing template surfaces as the engine's own {@link MustacheNotFoundException}
241    * (HTTP 500 from the renderer's catch-all); missing engine surfaces as
242    * {@link MustacheViewRenderer#NO_ENGINE_DIAGNOSTIC}.
243    *
244    * @param path The trailing path segment after {@code /mustache/} (the template name relative
245    *    to the configured {@link #getBasePath() base path}; configured suffix appended if
246    *    missing).
247    * @param req The current REST request.
248    * @param res The current REST response.
249    * @throws IOException If the underlying response writer fails.
250    * @throws BasicHttpException On boundary violation (403), missing engine (500), or render
251    *    failure (500).
252    */
253   @RestGet(
254      path="/${juneau.mustache.path:mustache}/*",
255      summary="Mustache view",
256      description="Render a raw Mustache template under the configured base path.",
257      swagger=@OpSwagger(ignore=true)
258   )
259   public void render(@Path("/*") String path, RestRequest req, RestResponse res)
260         throws IOException, BasicHttpException {
261      worker.render(path, req, res);
262   }
263
264   /**
265    * Builder for {@link MustacheMixin}.
266    *
267    * <p>
268    * Mirrors {@link MustacheDispatcher.Builder}'s configuration methods on its own surface and
269    * forwards each call into a held {@link MustacheDispatcher.Builder} (§2.3.1 worker-bean
270    * composition).
271    */
272   public static class Builder {
273
274      private final MustacheDispatcher.Builder worker = MustacheDispatcher.create();
275
276      /** Constructor &mdash; package access for {@link MustacheMixin#create()}. */
277      protected Builder() {}
278
279      /**
280       * Sets the classpath base path under which template resources are resolved.
281       *
282       * <p>
283       * Defaults to {@link MustacheMixin#DEFAULT_BASE_PATH "/"}. A typical configured
284       * value is {@code "/templates/"} &mdash; the same Spring-Boot-compatible layout
285       * convention the sibling JSP / Thymeleaf bridges use.
286       *
287       * @param value The base path. {@code null} or blank values reset to the default
288       *    {@link MustacheMixin#DEFAULT_BASE_PATH}.
289       * @return This object.
290       */
291      public Builder basePath(String value) {
292         worker.basePath(value);
293         return this;
294      }
295
296      /**
297       * Sets the suffix to append to template names that don't already end with it (idempotent).
298       *
299       * <p>
300       * Defaults to {@link MustacheMixin#DEFAULT_TEMPLATE_SUFFIX ""} (no implicit
301       * suffix &mdash; literal template names). Typical configured value is
302       * {@code ".mustache"}, the mustache.java convention.
303       *
304       * @param value The new suffix. {@code null} resets to
305       *    {@link MustacheMixin#DEFAULT_TEMPLATE_SUFFIX}.
306       * @return This object.
307       */
308      public Builder templateSuffix(String value) {
309         worker.templateSuffix(value);
310         return this;
311      }
312
313      /**
314       * Reads the current base path setting (test/inspection helper).
315       *
316       * @return The base path. Never {@code null}.
317       */
318      public String getBasePath() {
319         return worker.getBasePath();
320      }
321
322      /**
323       * Reads the current template-suffix setting (test/inspection helper).
324       *
325       * @return The template suffix. Never {@code null}.
326       */
327      public String getTemplateSuffix() {
328         return worker.getTemplateSuffix();
329      }
330
331      /**
332       * Builds the {@link MustacheMixin}.
333       *
334       * @return A new {@link MustacheMixin} instance.
335       */
336      public MustacheMixin build() {
337         return new MustacheMixin(this);
338      }
339   }
340}
